<findings>

[observation] [info] High cohesion (1) — files are tightly interconnected
[observation] [info] The zone is defined structurally by disconnection, not semantic grouping; any root-level file added without imports will silently land here regardless of its purpose.
[observation] [info] Three overlapping guidance documents (CLAUDE.md, CODEX.md, PACKAGE_GUIDELINES.md) may create onboarding confusion — a single index or README section linking all three would improve discoverability.
[observation] [info] Zero coupling is architecturally correct for the orchestration tier; cli.js and ci.js should never import domain packages directly.
[suggestion] [info] Rename 'project-root-config' to 'orchestration-root' or 'monorepo-root' to accurately reflect that it contains active orchestration scripts (ci.js, cli.js, web.js) and documentation alongside configuration files — the current name implies passive config and misleads contributors about the zone's operational role.

</findings>

<insights>

- High cohesion (1) — files are tightly interconnected
- This zone correctly enforces the orchestration tier — ci.js, cli.js, web.js spawn child processes rather than importing library code, keeping the dependency hierarchy clean.
- With 28 files and cohesion of 1.0 and coupling of 0.0, the Louvain detector is treating all unlinked root-level files as a single island; this is expected for a monorepo root but means the zone is structurally defined by absence of imports, not by semantic affinity.
- CLAUDE.md, CODEX.md, and PACKAGE_GUIDELINES.md coexist here — consider whether all three are discoverable to new contributors or whether one canonical entry point (e.g. README) should link them explicitly.
- Zero coupling is architecturally correct for the orchestration tier; cli.js and ci.js should never import domain packages directly.
- Three overlapping guidance documents (CLAUDE.md, CODEX.md, PACKAGE_GUIDELINES.md) may create onboarding confusion — a single index or README section linking all three would improve discoverability.
- The zone is defined structurally by disconnection, not semantic grouping; any root-level file added without imports will silently land here regardless of its purpose.
- Zone name 'project-root-config' implies passive configuration, but it contains active orchestration entry points (ci.js, cli.js, web.js); the name understates the zone's responsibility and may cause contributors to underestimate blast radius of changes here.
- ci.js is classified as an entrypoint within a zone named 'project-root-config' — the entrypoint classification and the zone name are semantically contradictory; entrypoints belong in an orchestration zone, not a config zone.
- Rename 'project-root-config' to 'orchestration-root' or 'monorepo-root' to accurately reflect that it contains active orchestration scripts (ci.js, cli.js, web.js) and documentation alongside configuration files — the current name implies passive config and misleads contributors about the zone's operational role.
- [call graph] 418 internal calls, 0 outgoing, 0 incoming (cohesion: 1, coupling: 0)

</insights>
